name: Ailill mac Máta
description: Ailill mac Máta was the king of Connacht in the Ulster Cycle of Irish mythology. He is best known as the husband of Queen Medb and co-ruler of Connacht. While Medb is often portrayed as the dominant figure, Ailill was a powerful king in his own right, commanding respect and influence. His lineage is less clearly defined than Medb's, but he is consistently presented as a noble and capable leader. He plays a crucial role in the Táin Bó Cúailnge (The Cattle Raid of Cooley), often acting as a foil to Medb's more aggressive and impulsive nature. Ailill is depicted as more pragmatic and strategic in his decision-making, sometimes attempting to temper Medb's ambitions. He is also known for his wealth and possessions, often displayed alongside Medb's in the epic tales. Despite his position and power, Ailill is ultimately overshadowed by Medb's legendary status, yet his presence is essential to understanding the dynamics of the Connacht court and the events of the Ulster Cycle. He is a significant figure, representing a more measured and politically astute approach to kingship within the tumultuous world of Irish myth. Ailill's character provides a contrast to the more overtly heroic figures like Cú Chulainn, offering a glimpse into the complexities of leadership and power within a tribal society. He is a complex character, not simply a king but a husband, a leader, and a strategist in a world filled with magic and war. He is ultimately killed by Conall Cernach.
subtitle: King of Connacht, husband of Medb.
achievements: Leading the forces of Connacht in war
Maintaining stability within the Connacht court
Participating in the Táin Bó Cúailnge

keyMyths.title: The Cattle Raid of Cooley (Táin Bó Cúailnge)
keyMyths.description: Ailill, along with Medb, launches a raid to steal the Brown Bull of Cooley, leading to a devastating war between Connacht and Ulster. His role is vital in the strategic planning and execution of the raid, though Medb often takes the lead.

keyMyths.title: The Pillow Talk
keyMyths.description: Ailill and Medb compare their wealth and possessions, leading to the realization that the only thing Medb lacks is the Brown Bull of Cooley, sparking the Táin. This scene highlights Ailill's wealth and the competitive dynamic between him and Medb.

keyMyths.title: Death at the hands of Conall Cernach
keyMyths.description: Ailill is eventually killed by Conall Cernach as revenge for the death of Fergus mac Róich. This marks the end of Ailill's reign and highlights the cyclical nature of violence and revenge in Irish mythology.

sources.author: Thomas Kinsella
sources.description: A modern translation and retelling of the Táin Bó Cúailnge.
sources.text: A modern translation and retelling of the Táin Bó Cúailnge.

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
sources.description: A collection of translations of early Irish myths, including stories featuring Ailill mac Máta.
